mcp_status.semantic_scholar: available — individual field queries returned results; cross-field queries returned zero hits (confirms disjointness)
mcp_status.pubmed: empty results for all queries (likely connectivity issue) — WebSearch used as primary fallback

candidates.title: Extreme Value Theory x Proteome Thermal Vulnerability Mapping
candidates.field_a: Extreme value statistics (GEV distributions, tail index analysis)
candidates.field_c: Proteome-wide thermal stability distributions (thermal proteome profiling)
candidates.adjacent_note: BLAST e-values use Gumbel distribution for sequence alignment scores — distinct application, NOT thermal stability distributions

papers_retrieved.title: Meltome atlas — thermal proteome stability across the tree of life
papers_retrieved.relevance: Largest Tm dataset (48,000 proteins, 13 species); identifies 20% unmeasured tail proteins; uses mean/SD statistics only — the EVT gap

papers_retrieved.title: Experimental and data analysis advances in thermal proteome profiling
papers_retrieved.relevance: Confirms statistical limitations of Tm-centric analysis; advocates GPMelt but not EVT; quantifies the 20% out-of-range problem

key_breakthroughs_field_a: GEV/EVT mature in hydrology, climate, finance, power grids — computationally tractable
Peaks-over-threshold and return level estimation standard practice for tail behavior
Fisher-Tippett-Gnedenko theorem: only 3 possible limit distributions for maxima
key_breakthroughs_field_c: Meltome Atlas (2020): 48,000 proteins across 13 species, Tm 30-90 C
20% of proteome has Tm outside measurable ranges — heavy tail problem
GPMelt (2024): Gaussian process alternative to curve fitting; still not EVT
All eukaryotes show substantial safety margin between OGT and bulk protein precipitation temperature
key_anomalies: 20% of proteome unmeasured because Tm values are outside temperature ranges — precisely the tail-estimation problem EVT addresses
Organism thermal death is set by extreme-vulnerable proteins (tail), not mean Tm — but field uses mean statistics
Safety margin between OGT and thermal death is a return-level problem — EVT is the correct tool but has never been applied

explored: BLAST Gumbel for sequence alignment scores
not_explored: GEV distribution fitting to proteome Tm data
Return level estimation for thermal death point prediction
Tail index classification across thermophiles vs mesophiles vs psychrophiles
Peaks-over-threshold for identifying thermally vulnerable subproteome
Fisher-Tippett-Gnedenko theorem to classify which EVT family a proteome belongs to
gap_analysis.most_promising: Apply GEV fitting to Meltome Atlas 48,000-protein dataset; compute return levels (temperature killing 1% of proteome); compare tail indices across thermal adaptation strategies

candidates.title: Reservoir Computing Theory x Gut Microbiome Community Dynamics
candidates.field_a: Reservoir computing (echo state networks, liquid state machines)
candidates.field_c: Gut microbiome temporal dynamics and resilience
candidates.adjacent_note: Bacterial RC (bioRxiv 2024) uses single-strain E. coli metabolic network as reservoir — NOT multi-species community dynamics. Ecological Jacobian stability analysis uses spectral radius of interaction matrix (same mathematical object as echo state property) but neither field has recognized the connection.

papers_retrieved.title: Ecological Modeling from Time-Series Inference: Insight into Dynamics and Stability of Intestinal Microbiota
papers_retrieved.relevance: Uses Jacobian spectral radius as stability criterion — mathematically identical to echo state property but never named as such; foundational paper showing the latent mathematical bridge

key_breakthroughs_field_a: Echo state networks: sparse recurrent reservoir + linear readout; edge of chaos at spectral radius ~ 1
Bacterial RC (bioRxiv Sept 2024): E. coli K-12 as physical reservoir for regression/classification tasks — single strain, metabolic network
Connectome-based ESN (2025): biological connectivity matrices improve overfitting resistance
Nature Communications RC review (2024): RC expanding to physical systems and edge AI
key_breakthroughs_field_c: gLV + Jacobian stability (Stein 2013): spectral radius |lambda_max(J)| < 1 = stable community — same criterion as echo state property
Graph neural networks for microbiome temporal prediction (Nature Comms 2025)
Alternative stable states in microbiome (Microbiome 2023): bistability consistent with edge-of-chaos dynamics
LSTM for microbiome community design (eLife): temporal prediction but not reservoir computing
key_anomalies: Spectral radius of interaction matrix is the key criterion in BOTH ecological stability theory AND echo state property — neither field has recognized this overlap
Dysbiosis (spectral radius > 1, unstable) maps to chaotic reservoir regime in RC theory — useful health biomarker that has never been computed from clinical microbiome data
LSTM models applied to microbiome dynamics but reservoir computing (which uses the biologically meaningful interaction matrix directly) has never been tried

explored: gLV dynamics + Jacobian stability for microbiome
Single-strain bacterial reservoir computing (E. coli metabolics)
not_explored: Multi-species community interaction matrix analyzed as reservoir weight matrix W
Echo state property formally computed for real microbiome interaction matrices
Memory capacity from fecal microbiota transplant (FMT) time series
Lyapunov exponent of community dynamics as health biomarker
Spectral radius ~ 1 as quantitative marker for resilient vs fragile microbiome
Input separability (dietary discrimination) as gut health metric via RC framework
gap_analysis.most_promising: Compute echo state property of inferred gLV interaction matrices from HMP/MITRE longitudinal datasets; test whether healthy subjects have spectral radius closer to 1 (edge of chaos) than IBD subjects

candidates.title: Granular Jamming Transition Physics x Chromatin Compaction During Confined Migration
candidates.field_a: Granular jamming physics (jamming transition, force chains, marginally jammed states)
candidates.field_c: Chromatin compaction mechanics during cancer cell constriction migration
candidates.adjacent_note: 'Nucleosome jamming' language exists in replication biology (kinetic parking-lot problem of nucleosome assembly on DNA) but is a distinct concept from the granular jamming transition (rigidity transition at phi_J). Cell-level jamming transitions in cancer metastasis exist but explicitly do not extend to sub-cellular chromatin organization. Chromatin glass/gel analogies exist but do not use jamming physics formalism.

papers_retrieved.title: Chromatin compaction during confined cell migration induces and reshapes nuclear condensates
papers_retrieved.authors: Zhao JZ, Xia J, Brangwynne CP
papers_retrieved.relevance: State-of-the-art chromatin mechanics during confinement; uses LLPS framework exclusively; no jamming physics formalism; confirms the gap

key_breakthroughs_field_a: Gardner transition coincides with jamming scalings in hard spheres (arXiv 2024): Gardner transition is precursor to jamming — structural order parameter established
Unjamming transition in cancer (PMID 39633605, 2024): cell-level critical shape index p0* = 3.81
Jamming in embryogenesis and cancer (Frontiers Physics 2021): tissue-level jamming
No biological applications at sub-cellular/chromatin scale in any paper found
key_breakthroughs_field_c: Zhao, Xia, Brangwynne 2024: LLPS framework for chromatin during confinement; no jamming formalism
PNAS 2025: chromatin decompaction stiffens nucleus — mechanical coupling confirmed
bioRxiv 2026: chromatin architecture and constriction cooperate in cancer dissemination
Multiscale chromatin condensates (Science 2025): LLPS and material properties
key_anomalies: Chromatin must undergo solid-to-fluid transition during nuclear squeezing — this is a jamming-unjamming transition at nucleosome packing level, but no paper computes phi_J for nucleosome arrays
Nucleosome volume fraction phi ~ 0.3-0.6 in heterochromatin is in the range where granular jamming predicts a phase transition
LLPS and glass/gel frameworks for chromatin are incompatible but both used — jamming theory could reconcile them

explored: Cell-level jamming transitions in cancer metastasis
Kinetic 'nucleosome jamming' in replication biology (parking-lot kinetics)
not_explored: Jamming transition phi_J for nucleosome packings in heterochromatin
Coordination number Z_c analysis for nucleosome contact networks
Force chain detection in compressed chromatin (high-resolution imaging + stress inference)
Gardner transition as origin of irreversible chromatin rearrangements during confined migration
Power-law elastic modulus scaling near jamming applied to chromatin mechanics data
gap_analysis.most_promising: Measure phi (nucleosome volume fraction) in heterochromatin vs euchromatin vs migrating cell constriction zones; test whether phi crosses phi_J during confinement; use sub-nuclear resolution force inference to detect force chains in jammed chromatin

candidates.title: ML-Augmented Acoustic Emission Classification x Plant Xylem Cavitation Mode Identification
candidates.field_a: ML-enhanced AE analysis (CWT+CNN) for composite failure mode classification
candidates.field_c: Plant xylem cavitation and embolism during drought stress
candidates.adjacent_note: Frequency-band feature clustering exists in plant AE literature (Vergeynst 2016) using partial powers in 100-200 kHz, 200-400 kHz, 400-800 kHz bands — analogous to composites NDT but rule-based, NOT CWT scalogram + CNN. The full deep learning pipeline transfer has never been made.

papers_retrieved.title: Deep Learning Approach for Damage Classification Based on Acoustic Emission Data in Composite Materials
papers_retrieved.relevance: CWT+CNN pipeline achieving 84.6-99.8% accuracy for composite failure mode classification — the source technology for transfer to plant AE

papers_retrieved.title: Time-frequency features of grapevine's xylem acoustic emissions for detection of drought stress
papers_retrieved.relevance: State of the art in plant AE: manual frequency-band feature extraction; no CNN/deep learning; represents the methodological gap the bridge would close

key_breakthroughs_field_a: CWT scalogram + CNN: 84.6-94.3% accuracy for composite damage mode classification (2018-2024 literature, mature pipeline)
InceptionTime on raw AE: 99.8% accuracy — but CWT+CNN remains dominant for interpretable feature spaces
ML for discrete AE review (Springer NDT Journal 2025): comprehensive; no plant applications mentioned
ASTM E1930 feature space well established for AE classification
key_breakthroughs_field_c: Vergeynst 2016: frequency-domain features for cavitation AE discrimination (foundational rule-based method)
Oletic 2020: time-frequency signatures for drought stress detection across 4 grapevine cultivars
Talk is Cheap review (Trends Plant Science 2024): plant acoustic emissions overview; ML potential noted but not implemented for cavitation mode classification
Revised acoustic vulnerability curves (Tree Physiology 2024): cumulative AE counting methods improved; still not source classification
key_anomalies: Plant AE source classification is formally identical to composite failure mode classification (multi-source discrimination from frequency/amplitude features) — but the transfer has never been made
Plant hydraulics literature argues AE is both a reliable drought indicator AND unreliable due to source ambiguity — deep learning source classification resolves this but has not been applied
CWT scalogram reduces data to interpretable 2D image that captures both time and frequency structure — precisely what noisy plant field AE data needs

explored: Manual frequency-band feature extraction for plant AE
Cumulative AE counting for hydraulic vulnerability curves
Basic wavelet scalar features (energy, entropy, variance)
not_explored: CWT scalogram generation from plant AE signals as CNN input
Transfer learning or domain adaptation from composites NDT models to plant AE
Multi-class source classification (cavitation vs bark cracking vs embolism propagation vs noise) using deep learning
Felicity ratio mapping to quantify cavitation reversibility (embolism refilling)
ASTM E1930 feature space applied to plant AE data
gap_analysis.most_promising: Train CWT+CNN on grapevine or oak dehydration AE data with known source proportions; validate against hydraulic conductance measurements; assess whether composite NDT domain adaptation provides useful prior features

candidates.title: Classical Nucleation Theory x Ferroptosis Ferritin Iron Pool Dynamics
candidates.field_a: Classical nucleation theory (nucleation kinetics, critical radius, supersaturation)
candidates.field_c: Intracellular iron pool dynamics during ferroptosis
candidates.adjacent_note: JACS 2025 papers on ferritin mineral nucleation use CNT concepts for ferritin assembly in HEALTHY cells — distinct from ferroptosis context. No paper applies CNT to iron speciation during ferroptosis or uses CNT to explain the LIP paradox.

papers_retrieved.title: Labile iron pool dynamics do not drive ferroptosis potentiation in colorectal cancer cells
papers_retrieved.relevance: Confirms the LIP paradox: LIP does NOT expand during ferroptosis despite ferritinophagy — the anomaly CNT is positioned to explain

key_breakthroughs_field_a: CNT nucleation landscape for biomolecular condensates (Nature 2021): CNT applies in cells for protein condensates
Ferritin mineral nucleation at 1.86 A resolution (JACS 2025): nucleation at acidic 3-fold pores, iron clusters resemble ferrihydrite — structural biology in healthy cells
Ferrihydrite transformation kinetics: labile Fe(III) supersaturation controls nucleation consistent with CNT principles (Geochimica 2021)
LSW Ostwald ripening kinetics: well-established framework for nano-particle coarsening applicable to iron nano-clusters
key_breakthroughs_field_c: Ponnusamy et al. 2025: LIP does NOT expand during ferroptosis despite ferritinophagy — central paradox of the field
Canonical ferroptosis mechanism review (Kong 2024): ferritinophagy → Fe3+ release → LIP expansion → Fenton → lipid peroxidation — CONTRADICTED by 2025 finding
NRF2 controls iron homeostasis during ferroptosis (Science Advances 2023)
Iron metabolism and ferroptosis review (PMC12374342, 2024): no nucleation theory content
key_anomalies: LIP paradox (Ponnusamy 2025): ferritinophagy releases iron from ferritin but LIP probe does not detect free Fe2+ increase — iron must be in a form the probe cannot detect

CNT prediction: at intracellular pH ~7.2 and the iron concentrations released by ferritinophagy, iron supersaturation drives nucleation of sub-critical Fe(OH)3/ferrihydrite clusters below detection threshold of TRX-PURO probe
pH drops during ferroptosis (acidification): lower pH → smaller r* → more nucleation → less free LIP → explains the paradox kinetically
key_contradictions: Field holds simultaneously: (a) ferritinophagy is required for ferroptosis and (b) LIP does not expand during ferroptosis. CNT resolves this: iron is released but immediately re-nucleates as sub-critical clusters.

not_explored: CNT nucleation rate equation J = A*exp(-DeltaG*/kT) applied to iron speciation during ferroptosis
Critical nucleus radius r* as function of intracellular pH during ferroptosis
Ostwald ripening kinetics (LSW theory) for iron nano-clusters during ferritinophagy
Ferrihydrite surface energy gamma in cytoplasmic conditions at ferroptotic pH
Mineral dissolution rate law connecting ferritin iron release to free LIP at cytoplasmic pH
Sub-critical iron nano-cluster detection by methods beyond free Fe2+ probes
gap_analysis.most_promising: Compute expected LIP probe signal under CNT-predicted nano-cluster formation during ferroptosis; test whether alkaline conditions (larger r*, less nucleation) allow LIP expansion and accelerate ferroptosis; use iron speciation techniques beyond TRX-PURO (XANES, Mossbauer on ferroptotic cells)

highest_confidence.reason: Zero cross-field papers; adjacent BLAST Gumbel application is distinct

lowest_confidence.reason: Frequency-band clustering exists in plant AE (Vergeynst) — bridge-level disjoint but not field-level

overall_disjointness_summary.note: All 5 candidates classified DISJOINT or DISJOINT_AT_BRIDGE_LEVEL. All 5 are recommended for Generator phase.
